## Root cause — found and fixed

**Every** "Hook JSON output validation failed — (root): Invalid input" over the past month traces to a single hook: **`~/.claude/hooks/tdd-guard.py`**, which was emitting Claude Code's *stale* output schema.

**Exhaustive count (2026-05-17 → 06-15, all 1,522 recent JSONLs):**

| Malformed payload | Occurrences | Where in tdd-guard.py |
|---|---|---|
| `{"decision": "allow"}` | **2,477** | 7 silent-allow sites |
| `{"decision": "allow", "message": ...}` | **162** | TDD NOTICE + WARNING |
| *(echoes, no payload)* | 51 | duplicate references |
| **Total** | **2,690** across 127 sessions / 17 repos | |

The problem: current Claude Code only accepts `decision: "approve" | "block"` — `"allow"` was moved out of the top level, and `"message"` was never a valid key. So the hook *meant* to allow, the harness rejected the JSON as malformed, and you got noise on essentially every Write/Edit. It ramped hard in June (344 on the 4th, ~280 on the 13th–14th) because tdd-guard got Swift support on 06-06 and you've been editing lots of impl files since.

Every **other** hook was already correct (`{}` for allow, `{"decision":"block","reason":...}` for blocks) — tdd-guard was the lone holdout.

## The fix (applied + empirically verified)

- 7× silent-allow → `{}` (matches every sibling hook; preserves normal permission flow)
- TDD NOTICE/WARNING → `{"systemMessage": "..."}` — valid **and** these nudges will now actually *display* for the first time (they were being silently rejected before)
- BLOCK → `message` renamed to `reason` (the valid field)

I ran the patched hook against **all 10 code paths** (env-disabled, worker, non-Write, no-path, non-impl, test-file, new-file NOTICE, 1st-edit WARNING, 3rd-edit BLOCK, has-test) — **all 10 produce schema-valid output**. Backup saved at `tdd-guard.py.bak-prevalidationfix`.

1. **`collab-guard.py`** — the 50 "PreToolUse:Write hook error" events are *deliberate* exit-2 blocks ("Collab file missing mandatory sections"), working as designed. Not malformed; possibly over-firing if it annoys you — I can tune it if you want.
2. **`block-dangerous-commands.py`** false-positive — it blocked one of my read-only analysis scripts because the word "trunc­ated" appeared in a *comment* (matched the SQL `TRUNC` pattern). Same over-firing family. Easy fix to anchor that pattern to real SQL.

Assistant: ## Enrichment diagnosis — and yes, it was effectively shut off (for a fixable reason)

**Your question "is it not running on purpose?":** It *was* turned off — but not for a reason that should stick. Here's what's actually happening:

- The memory database allows **only one writer at a time** (a hard lock).
- The "hotlane" added recently (WI-4) — the thing that makes a new memory instantly searchable — runs as an always-on process that **grabs that single writer slot and holds it 24/7**. But it **only embeds; it never enriches**.

**Plain-English update (for Etan):**

Quick follow-up on the second-account memory-walls test. The builder fixed tonight's problem fast, and I checked the fix myself (didn't just take their word — I re-ran their tests and traced the code). **The fix is correct.** But I'm calling it a *conditional* pass, not a full pass, for two honest reasons you'll want to know:

1. **The fix makes per-agent walls *possible*, but not yet *automatic*.** Each agent now *can* say "I'm a worker" or "I'm the boss" when it asks the memory — but nothing yet *makes* each agent say that on every request. If an agent forgets to, it falls back to the old shared setting. So the real remaining work is a small "name tag" layer: each agent type needs to automatically stamp its role on every memory lookup. **This is the thing the second-account setup guide must spell out** — otherwise the walls quietly won't apply to real agents.

2. **The fix isn't loaded into the live memory server yet.** It's sitting in a code branch. A true end-to-end test with real agents only means something once that branch is actually running. That's a server restart that touches every agent currently working, so I deliberately did **not** do it tonight. The clean moment is as the first step of tomorrow's dry-run — then I'll re-run the exact two-agent test and confirm the walls hold for real.

**Bottom line, unchanged:** none of this blocks creating the second account tomorrow — that account gets its own separate memory database, so its walls are physically real regardless. The builder is now moving on to the bigger "lead sees its worktrees" piece, and I'll re-test everything once the code is actually deployed. No decision needed from you — I'm running the test-and-retest loop and will report when it's genuinely green on real agents.

chunk_text:
VoiceLayer paste-wedge fix + cmuxlayer streaming convergence (PR #280, 2026-06-14, gen16).

CORRECTION (surprise): The CLAUDE.md "Paste strategy: CGEvent Cmd+V" note is STALE. VoiceBar transcript auto-paste is Accessibility-insertion-only (clipboard/Cmd+V paths were removed in an in-flight refactor). Verify code before trusting any paste note.

ROOT CAUSE of "VoiceLayer breaks panes via how it pastes": CommandModeAXHelper.insertText read the ENTIRE focused element's kAXValueAttribute, string-replaced, then wrote the WHOLE value back via one atomic AXUIElementSetAttributeValue(kAXValue). For a terminal pane the AX value IS the whole buffer, so the wholesale rewrite wedged the pane.

FIX (Part A — kills wedge): insertAtCursor now uses the surgical kAXSelectedTextAttribute primitive (insert at caret / replace selection with ONLY the new text — never reads/rewrites the full value). Guarded fallback to legacy value-rewrite ONLY when kAXSelectedText fails AND value < 5000 chars; large values bail (never a big rewrite). Injectable seams for tests.

FIX (Part B — cmuxlayer convergence): wordChunks (spacing-preserving) + streamInsertText stream word-by-word (18ms/word via the pasteScheduler seam), single-shot for short text, mid-stream miss handling. Adopted from cmuxlayer/src/server.ts deliverInputChunks + chunkTerminalInput (SEND_INPUT_CHUNK_DELAY_MS=5, threshold 500, line-aligned chunks, ack-waited, pasted-not-keystroked) — post-PR#156.

VERIFICATION: swift build exit 0; swift test = 352 tests, 0 failures. Reproduction-in-test: testInsertAtCursorUsesSelectedTextWriterAndNeverRewritesWholeValue asserts the whole-value writer is NEVER called when kAXSelectedText succeeds; plus large-value guard + 7 word-stream tests.

PROCESS LEARNINGS: (1) Codex worker hung on model-load (TUI up, "model: loading" persists, input rejected) — fall back to a Claude worker rather than fight it. (2) A background Claude worker left orphan swift-test/xctest processes (PPID 1) contending on the SwiftPM .build lock → fresh runs die exit-1 with no output or block "Another instance of SwiftPM is already running"; kill orphans (pkill xctest/swiftpm-testing-helper) before re-running. (3) A tail -F collab monitor re-emits historical content when the channel file is rotated/regenerated (clx-core journal work) — use an offset-tracking poll loop (wc -l baseline, sed new lines, reset on shrink) instead; and case-insensitive "blocked" matches prose/"unblocked" (use uppercase \bBLOCKED\b).

GATES REMAINING at PR time: daemon runtime-verify (.verified/ artifact — checkpoint commit touched SocketServer.swift shouldRouteClientEvent event-routing) + UI-demo video (dictate long text into terminal pane → pane survives + words stream) on a NON-resident dogfood build (live-disruption rule: never rebuild/swap the resident VoiceBar.app while Etan uses it). Both need Etan's live app+mic window.
